title: Lophiaris carthagenensis
taxonomy: Lophiaris carthagenensis is a species of orchid in the subtribe Oncidiinae. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to South America. The genus Lophiaris is a monotypic genus, meaning it is composed of a single species. Lophiaris carthagenensis is often confused with other orchid species due to its similar appearance. Its classification is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions.

intro: Lophiaris carthagenensis, also known as Cat's Claw, is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest and other parts of Central and South America, with its name derived from the hook-like thorns on its stem. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and anxiety, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lophiaris carthagenensis include alkaloids, such as isothalifoline, oxindole alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cat's Claw is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often marketed as supplements for immune system support and overall well-being.
